annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58446,"title":"[Tables] Docs: Add GetEntityAsync/GetEntityIfExistsAsync examples and error-handling guidance to README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Data.Tables` **Service directory:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/` **Triggered by:** commit 8aeb2a21d (PR #58305) by `@jsquire` ## What Changed PR #58305 fixed a bug where `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` could throw `ArgumentNullException` instead of `RequestFailedException` when the HTTP response had a null or empty `ContentStream`. The fix ensures these methods now consistently surface `RequestFailedException` for all error conditions, conforming to the SDK error-handling contract. ## Gaps Found - `GetEntityAsync` and `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` are entirely absent from the README's **Examples** section — there is no "Get a single entity" example showing how to fetch an entity by partition key and row key - The **Troubleshooting** section only covers `RequestFailedException` for a duplicate-table create conflict; it does not demonstrate catching `RequestFailedException` when retrieving an entity (e.g., 404 Not Found) - No test-backed snippet exists for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, making it harder for the snippet tooling to inject a live example into the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files No existing files need to be removed or restructured; the changes are additions only. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### Snippet A — Get a single entity (async) **Test file to create or extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` Add a new test method (or add to an existing `Sample4_*` file) that demonstrates `GetEntityAsync`. Wrap the sample code in a named region: ```csharp [Test] public async Task GetEntityAsync() { // ... setup: create table and add an entity ... #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync // Fetch a single entity by its partition key and row key. TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync( partitionKey=[REDACTED] rowKey=[REDACTED] Console.WriteLine($"Product: {entity.GetString("Product")}, Price: {entity.GetDouble("Price")}"); #endregion } ``` **README location:** Add the new snippet under the `## Examples` → `### Query table entities` section (or add a new `### Get a table entity` subsection before that section), referencing: ````markdown ```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityAsync ``` ```` #### Snippet B — Get entity if exists (async) **Test file to create or extend:** same `Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` file ```csharp [Test] public async Task GetEntityIfExistsAsync() { // ... setup ... #region Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync // Try to fetch a single entity; returns null if not found rather than throwing. NullableResponse response = await tableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync( partitionKey=[REDACTED] rowKey=[REDACTED] if (response.HasValue) { Console.WriteLine($"Found: {response.Value.RowKey}"); } else { Console.WriteLine("Entity not found."); } #endregion } ``` **README location:** Immediately after the `GetEntityAsync` snippet. ````markdown ```C# Snippet:TablesSample4GetEntityIfExistsAsync ``` ```` #### Snippet C — RequestFailedException when getting an entity (troubleshooting) **Test file to extend:** `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample7_Troubleshooting.cs` Add a new test method demonstrating error handling for `GetEntityAsync`: ```csharp [Test] public async Task GetEntityNotFoundAsync() { // ... setup ... #region Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync try { TableEntity entity = await tableClient.GetEntityAsync( partitionKey=[REDACTED] rowKey=[REDACTED] } catch (RequestFailedException ex) when (ex.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound) { Console.WriteLine($"Entity not found: {ex.Message}"); } #endregion } ``` **README location:** Add under `## Troubleshooting`, after the existing `CreateDuplicateTable` snippet, with a short introductory sentence: > To check whether an entity exists without an exception, use `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`. When using `GetEntityAsync`, a `RequestFailedException` with status `404` is thrown if the entity does not exist. ````markdown ```C# Snippet:GetEntityNotFoundAsync ``` ```` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all six required top-level sections. The sections that need content added are: | Section | Status | |---------|--------| | Examples | Missing `GetEntityAsync` / `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` examples | | Troubleshooting | Only covers table creation conflict; missing entity retrieval error handling | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tables` — confirm README receives the new snippet content 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 tables` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 5. `dotnet format sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24730406234/agentic_workflow) · ● 913.6K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58446","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-21T15:21:50Z","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:21:50Z"}

Unable to use StringComparison with table query","body":"### Library name and version Azure.Data.Tables 12.10.0 ### Describe the bug As of v12.10.0 of Azure.Data.Tables, I'm unable to use any string comparison methods with a `StringComparison` argument passed in for the query filter. `Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.QueryAsync(Expression> filter, ...)` `tableClient.QueryAsync(t => t.PartitionKey.Equals(partitionKey, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))` `tableClient.QueryAsync(t => string.Compare(t.PartitionKey, partitionKey, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) == 0)` Both approaches throw an exception as it is reported that the string equals and comparison methods taking in a `StringComparison` argument are not supported but they were in 12.9.x. Currently I have only tested with `StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase` as that is my use case and the error does not indicate an issue with that specific `StringComparison` but with the number of arguments being passed in. I will, however, do some testing and update this issue if I notice a difference in behaviour with other `StringComparison` values. ### Expected behavior The expected behaviour is that the string comparison is evaluated with regards to the `StringComparison` argument passed in as it did in v12.9.x ### Actual behavior An exception is thrown if a `StringComparison` argument is provided. Given two strings, `x` and `y`, and a `StringComparison comp`, In 12.9.0 & 12.9.1: `x.Equals(y, comp)` works without issue `string.Compare(x, y, comparison) == 0` works without issue In 12.10.0: `x.Equals(y, comp)` => "Equals method with more than two arguments is not supported." `string.Compare(x, y, comp) == 0` => "string.Compare method with more than two arguments is not supported." Also to mention, `string.Equals(x, y, comp)` doesn't work in any of the versions listed. In 12.9.x the error message is "Incorrect number of arguments supplied for call to method 'Boolean Equals(System.String, System.String, System.StringComparison)' (Parameter 'method')", but in 12.10.0 ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.Data.Tables` — specifically missing README examples for `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync` and missing troubleshooting guidance for `RequestFailedException` on entity retrieval, triggered by a recent bug fix in PR #58305.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Data.Tables`
**Affected API:** `TableClient.GetEntityAsync`, `TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync`
**Scenarios:**
- No "Get a single entity" example in the README Examples section
- Troubleshooting section only covers duplicate-table creation; missing entity retrieval error handling (404 Not Found)
- No test-backed snippet for `GetEntityIfExistsAsync` for snippet tooling injection

**Root ask:** Add code snippets and README documentation for `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync` including error-handling guidance.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation improvement request triggered by commit `8aeb2a21d` (PR #58305), which fixed `GetEntityAsync`/`GetEntityIfExistsAsync` to throw `RequestFailedException` instead of `ArgumentNullException` on null/empty `ContentStream`.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/README.md` to confirm gaps in Examples and Troubleshooting sections
2. Add snippet test methods to `sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/tests/samples/Sample4_QueryEntitiesAsync.cs` and `Sample7_Troubleshooting.cs`
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 tables` to inject snippets into the README
4. Run `dotnet test sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — Issue is about SDK code/documentation behavior, not management plane or provisioning
- **Service:** `Tables` — Package is `Azure.Data.Tables` in `/sdk/tables/`
- **Confidence:** High — Package and service directory are explicitly named; multiple quality reference issues (e.g., #49847, #48745) confirm `Client` + `Tables` pairing
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Tables` (line 490) — service label "Tables" matches
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @christothes, @jorgerangel-msft
- **ServiceOwners:** @PaulCheng, @sakash279, @sivethe, @ThomasWeiss
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@christothes` (randomly selected from multiple AzureSdkOwners); "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported)
